steps.title: Use the persuadable clue
steps.content: The drivers already strongly believe the toll is too high, and the next sentence says advocates should target a more persuadable group. That means these drivers are unlikely to accept arguments for raising the toll.

steps.title: Match opposition to arguments
steps.content: Choice A means referenced in, Choice B means informed of, and Choice D means calmed by. None captures resistance to the pro-increase arguments. The answer is C.
